Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria), mild hypoglycemia, and abnormal liver enzyme (ALT). Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, and proteinuria with low specific gravity and brown, cloudy urine, suggesting possible diabetic ketoacidosis and nephropathy. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Immediate clinical evaluation and management are recommended.
